ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 中村精貮『志摩の地名の話』伊勢志摩国立公園協会、昭和26th year 11月3日、167p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}}

revision.comment: 15 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:草奈伎神社 up to rev 88827821
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 草奈伎神社 |画像 = [[File:Kusanagi-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]常磐一丁目5番 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 41.4 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 41.9 |祭神 = [[御剣仗神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
[[豊受大神宮]]摂社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月19日]]） |神事 = [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか }} {{神社 |名称 = 大間国生神社 |画像 = [[File:Omakunari-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 三重県伊勢市常磐一丁目5番 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline}} |祭神 = [[大幡主命|大若子命]]・[[乙若子命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
[[豊受大神宮]]摂社 |創建 = 伝[[雄略天皇]]21年（[[477年]]） |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 神嘗祭大間巡回（10月19日） |神事 = [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか }} '''草奈伎神社'''（くさなぎじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[豊受大神宮]]（[[外宮]]）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。外宮の摂社としては第1位である伊勢文化舎（2008）：53ページ。本記事では草奈伎神社と同じ社地にある外宮摂社の'''大間国生神社'''（おおまくなりじんじゃ）についても記述する。 草奈伎神社・大間国生神社ともに外宮の中でも格式の高い[[神社]]であり、[[神宮式年遷宮|20年に1度の社殿の造り替え]]が行われてきたほか、他の摂社以下の神社に比べ多くの[[祭祀]]が行われる櫻井（1969）：91ページ。 == 概要 == ここでは草奈伎神社・大間国生神社共通事項について記述する。 [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]常磐一丁目1142番地式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ（[[住居表示]]では常磐一丁目5番）、大間広と呼ばれる[[台地]]上に鎮座する。一の鳥居から宮域に入り、向かって左側（西側）が草奈伎神社、右側（東側）が大間国生神社である。宮域[[面積]]は1,744[[平方メートル|m2]]式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ。地元では氏神の祭礼当日に小俣神社へ参拝する[[風習]]がある櫻井（1991）：294ページ。 [[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。鎮座地は南側が伊勢市道に面するほかは三方を[[住宅地]]に囲まれている。 == 祭神 == 草奈伎神社の[[祭神]]は御剣仗神（みしるしのつるぎのかみ）。外宮の祭祀を司ってきた[[度会氏]]の遠祖である[[大幡主命|大若子命]]（おおわかこのみこと、おおわくごのみこと、大幡主命）が[[垂仁天皇|垂仁朝]]に[[越国]]の阿彦（あびこ）を平定する際に使った剣の[[霊]]であるという。大若子命は大間国生神社に祀られている。[[皇大神宮]]（内宮）方の[[学者]]側からは、大若子命を度会氏の遠祖に位置付けることが不遜であると非難されているが、[[櫻井勝之進]]は「度会の一族がかつて祖神と仰いだ神々であったということだけは尊重してよいと思われる」と述べている。 大間国生神社の祭神は先述の大若子命と[[乙若子命]]（おとわかこのみこと、おとわくごのみこと）の兄弟。両神とも[[京都市]]にある[[梅宮大社]]の祭神と同じであるとされる式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ。大若子命は[[鳥羽市]]沖の[[答志島]]・和具の[[古墳]]に、乙若子命は[[志摩市]][[志摩町和具]]の古墳にも葬られていると伝承されている中村（1951）：152ページ。 == 草奈伎神社 == 社名・草奈伎神社の「くさなぎ」の表記は『止由氣儀式帳』では「草奈支」、『神名秘書』では「草奈岐」となっており、[[近世]]には先述の2つに加え「草薙」も出現したが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』にしたがって今日では「草奈伎」に統一されている式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ。[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の[[伝説]]にちなんで命名されたという説がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：215ページ。 [[社殿]]は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]で、写真にあるように[[玉垣]]に囲まれている式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ。[[承元]]4年（[[1210年]]）にそれまで外宮摂社の第1位であった[[月夜見宮]]が別宮に昇格して以降、草奈伎神社が摂社の第1位となった。[[1976年]]（[[昭和]]51年）[[6月25日]]に建て替えられた。 == 大間国生神社 == 『倭姫命世記』 によれば[[倭姫命]]が[[雄略天皇]]21年[[丁巳]]冬10月（[[ユリウス暦]]：[[477年]]10月 - 11月）に定めた神社であるとされ、社名・大間国生神社は、「大間社」と記す[[古書]]もある式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ。外宮の摂社としては第2位である。 社殿は草奈伎神社と同様に[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]である式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ。1つの[[玉垣]]の中に2つの[[社殿]]が建っており、右側（東側）が大間社で大若子命を、左側（西側）が国生社で乙若子命を祀るとされる。『[[勢陽五鈴遺響]]』に同様の記述がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：301ページ。式内社であり、多くの古書に2つの社殿からなることが記されているが、『延喜式神名帳』では「大間国生神社」とあるのみで2つの社殿があるとは記されていない式内社研究会 編（1990）：303ページ。1976年（昭和51年）[[5月28日]]に建て替えられた。 『類聚神祇本源』では[[清野井庭神社]]が「大間社東野」にあり、草名伎社が「大間社西」にあったと記述しており、清野井庭神社を今社に比定する説が正しいとすれば、大間国生神社と草奈伎神社の位置がずれることになる式内社研究会 編（1990）：330ページ。この問題に関して『宇治山田市史』では、往古の大間国生神社は[[江戸時代]]の同社よりも広い社地を持っていたと考えられるが、清野井庭神社の再興時点での大間国生神社の社地を基準に考えてしまったため現社地（伊勢市常磐一丁目8番）に清野井庭神社が鎮座したのであり、今社（伊勢市宮町一丁目1番）が式内社の清野井庭神社の本来の社地であるとしている宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：下巻936 - 937ページ。 == 歴史 == ここでは草奈伎神社・大間国生神社共通事項について記述する。 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『延喜式神名帳』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された。また、『止由氣儀式帳』にも記載がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 216ページことから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。[[応仁]]2年（[[1469年]]）にはまだ存続していたものの[[中世]]に廃絶し、近世には社地不明となる式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 216, 217, 302, 304ページ。 [[禰宜]]度会常晨（桧垣常晨）の尽力により、[[正保]]2年（[[1645年]]）に現社地で再興された。現社地が[[古代]]の草奈伎神社社地と同じであるかどうかについては近世に議論もあったが、遷座することはなかった式内社研究会 編（1990）：213 - 215ページ。多くの摂社が再興された[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）には、すでに再興されていた草奈伎神社・大間国生神社では社殿の建て替えと宮域の画定が行われた式内社研究会 編（1990）：215 - 216, 300 - 301ページ。 大間国生神社は、[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[7月29日]]、[[宇治山田空襲]]により被災した伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ。 == 祭祀 == 草奈伎神社では[[歳旦祭]]（1月）、[[元始祭]]（1月）、建国記念祭（[[2月11日]]）、[[祈年祭]]（2月）、風日祈祭（5月・8月）、[[月次祭]]（6月・12月）、[[神嘗祭]]（10月）、[[新嘗祭]]（11月）、[[天長祭]]（[[12月23日]]）のすべてが境内にて祭祀を執行しており、外宮の摂社では唯一遥祀によらず祭儀が行われる式内社研究会 編（1990）：216ページ。同じ宮域にある大間国生神社では歳旦祭、元始祭、建国記念祭、風日祈祭、天長祭を遥祀により、その他の祭典は社殿の前で巡回祭祀により執行する。 == 植物相 == 境内には[[高木]]の[[ケヤキ]]、[[ムクノキ]]、[[クスノキ]]、[[カシ]]のほか中低木の[[アラカシ]]、[[ネズミモチ]]、[[サンゴジュ]]が茂る式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ。境内地の[[生垣]]にはネズミモチ、サンゴジュと[[イスノキ]]が植えられている式内社研究会 編（1990）：217, 303ページ。 == 交通 == ; 公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]] [[山田上口駅]]の南南東約200[[メートル|m]]のところに鎮座する。ほど近くに外宮摂社の[[清野井庭神社]]がある。 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]] [[宮町駅]]より徒歩で約9分（約750m）。 * [[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]「山田上口」[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩で約2分（約170m）。 ; 自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]] [[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約8分（約3.4[[キロメートル|km]]）。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和44年5月20日、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 中村精貮『志摩の地名の話』伊勢志摩国立公園協会、昭和26年11月3日、167p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0602.html 草奈伎神社（くさなぎじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0603.html 大間国生神社（おおまくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:くさなきしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]]

[[豊受大神宮]]摂社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月19日]]） |神事 = [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか }} {{神社 |名称 = 大間国生神社 |画像 = [[File:Omakunari-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 三重県伊勢市常磐一丁目5番 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline}} |祭神 = [[大幡主命|大若子命]]・[[乙若子命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
[[豊受大神宮]]摂社 |創建 = 伝[[雄略天皇]]21年（[[477年]]） |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 神嘗祭大間巡回（10月19日） |神事 = [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか }} '''草奈伎神社'''（くさなぎじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[豊受大神宮]]（[[外宮]]）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。外宮の摂社としては第1位である伊勢文化舎（2008）：53ページ。本記事では草奈伎神社と同じ社地にある外宮摂社の'''大間国生神社'''（おおまくなりじんじゃ）についても記述する。 草奈伎神社・大間国生神社ともに外宮の中でも格式の高い[[神社]]であり、[[神宮式年遷宮|20年に1度の社殿の造り替え]]が行われてきたほか、他の摂社以下の神社に比べ多くの[[祭祀]]が行われる櫻井（1969）：91ページ。 == 概要 == ここでは草奈伎神社・大間国生神社共通事項について記述する。 [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]常磐一丁目1142番地式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ（[[住居表示]]では常磐一丁目5番）、大間広と呼ばれる[[台地]]上に鎮座する。一の鳥居から宮域に入り、向かって左側（西側）が草奈伎神社、右側（東側）が大間国生神社である。宮域[[面積]]は1,744[[平方メートル|m2]]式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ。地元では氏神の祭礼当日に小俣神社へ参拝する[[風習]]がある櫻井（1991）：294ページ。 [[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。鎮座地は南側が伊勢市道に面するほかは三方を[[住宅地]]に囲まれている。 == 祭神 == 草奈伎神社の[[祭神]]は御剣仗神（みしるしのつるぎのかみ）。外宮の祭祀を司ってきた[[度会氏]]の遠祖である[[大幡主命|大若子命]]（おおわかこのみこと、おおわくごのみこと、大幡主命）が[[垂仁天皇|垂仁朝]]に[[越国]]の阿彦（あびこ）を平定する際に使った剣の[[霊]]であるという。大若子命は大間国生神社に祀られている。[[皇大神宮]]（内宮）方の[[学者]]側からは、大若子命を度会氏の遠祖に位置付けることが不遜であると非難されているが、[[櫻井勝之進]]は「度会の一族がかつて祖神と仰いだ神々であったということだけは尊重してよいと思われる」と述べている。 大間国生神社の祭神は先述の大若子命と[[乙若子命]]（おとわかこのみこと、おとわくごのみこと）の兄弟。両神とも[[京都市]]にある[[梅宮大社]]の祭神と同じであるとされる式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ。大若子命は[[鳥羽市]]沖の[[答志島]]・和具の[[古墳]]に、乙若子命は[[志摩市]][[志摩町和具]]の古墳にも葬られていると伝承されている中村（1951）：152ページ。 == 草奈伎神社 == 社名・草奈伎神社の「くさなぎ」の表記は『止由氣儀式帳』では「草奈支」、『神名秘書』では「草奈岐」となっており、[[近世]]には先述の2つに加え「草薙」も出現したが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』にしたがって今日では「草奈伎」に統一されている式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ。[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の[[伝説]]にちなんで命名されたという説がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：215ページ。 [[社殿]]は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]で、写真にあるように[[玉垣]]に囲まれている式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ。[[承元]]4年（[[1210年]]）にそれまで外宮摂社の第1位であった[[月夜見宮]]が別宮に昇格して以降、草奈伎神社が摂社の第1位となった。[[1976年]]（[[昭和]]51年）[[6月25日]]に建て替えられた。 == 大間国生神社 == 『倭姫命世記』 によれば[[倭姫命]]が[[雄略天皇]]21年[[丁巳]]冬10月（[[ユリウス暦]]：[[477年]]10月 - 11月）に定めた神社であるとされ、社名・大間国生神社は、「大間社」と記す[[古書]]もある式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ。外宮の摂社としては第2位である。 社殿は草奈伎神社と同様に[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]である式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ。1つの[[玉垣]]の中に2つの[[社殿]]が建っており、右側（東側）が大間社で大若子命を、左側（西側）が国生社で乙若子命を祀るとされる。『[[勢陽五鈴遺響]]』に同様の記述がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：301ページ。式内社であり、多くの古書に2つの社殿からなることが記されているが、『延喜式神名帳』では「大間国生神社」とあるのみで2つの社殿があるとは記されていない式内社研究会 編（1990）：303ページ。1976年（昭和51年）[[5月28日]]に建て替えられた。 『類聚神祇本源』では[[清野井庭神社]]が「大間社東野」にあり、草名伎社が「大間社西」にあったと記述しており、清野井庭神社を今社に比定する説が正しいとすれば、大間国生神社と草奈伎神社の位置がずれることになる式内社研究会 編（1990）：330ページ。この問題に関して『宇治山田市史』では、往古の大間国生神社は[[江戸時代]]の同社よりも広い社地を持っていたと考えられるが、清野井庭神社の再興時点での大間国生神社の社地を基準に考えてしまったため現社地（伊勢市常磐一丁目8番）に清野井庭神社が鎮座したのであり、今社（伊勢市宮町一丁目1番）が式内社の清野井庭神社の本来の社地であるとしている宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：下巻936 - 937ページ。 == 歴史 == ここでは草奈伎神社・大間国生神社共通事項について記述する。 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『延喜式神名帳』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された。また、『止由氣儀式帳』にも記載がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 216ページことから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。[[応仁]]2年（[[1469年]]）にはまだ存続していたものの[[中世]]に廃絶し、近世には社地不明となる式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 216, 217, 302, 304ページ。 [[禰宜]]度会常晨（桧垣常晨）の尽力により、[[正保]]2年（[[1645年]]）に現社地で再興された。現社地が[[古代]]の草奈伎神社社地と同じであるかどうかについては近世に議論もあったが、遷座することはなかった式内社研究会 編（1990）：213 - 215ページ。多くの摂社が再興された[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）には、すでに再興されていた草奈伎神社・大間国生神社では社殿の建て替えと宮域の画定が行われた式内社研究会 編（1990）：215 - 216, 300 - 301ページ。 大間国生神社は、[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[7月29日]]、[[宇治山田空襲]]により被災した伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ。 == 祭祀 == 草奈伎神社では[[歳旦祭]]（1月）、[[元始祭]]（1月）、建国記念祭（[[2月11日]]）、[[祈年祭]]（2月）、風日祈祭（5月・8月）、[[月次祭]]（6月・12月）、[[神嘗祭]]（10月）、[[新嘗祭]]（11月）、[[天長祭]]（[[12月23日]]）のすべてが境内にて祭祀を執行しており、外宮の摂社では唯一遥祀によらず祭儀が行われる式内社研究会 編（1990）：216ページ。同じ宮域にある大間国生神社では歳旦祭、元始祭、建国記念祭、風日祈祭、天長祭を遥祀により、その他の祭典は社殿の前で巡回祭祀により執行する。 == 植物相 == 境内には[[高木]]の[[ケヤキ]]、[[ムクノキ]]、[[クスノキ]]、[[カシ]]のほか中低木の[[アラカシ]]、[[ネズミモチ]]、[[サンゴジュ]]が茂る式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ。境内地の[[生垣]]にはネズミモチ、サンゴジュと[[イスノキ]]が植えられている式内社研究会 編（1990）：217, 303ページ。 == 交通 == ; 公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]] [[山田上口駅]]の南南東約200[[メートル|m]]のところに鎮座する。ほど近くに外宮摂社の[[清野井庭神社]]がある。 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]] [[宮町駅]]より徒歩で約9分（約750m）。 * [[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]「山田上口」[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩で約2分（約170m）。 ; 自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]] [[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約8分（約3.4[[キロメートル|km]]）。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和44年5月20日、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|12=draft}}・{{ill|Omakunari Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kusanagi-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-5 Tokiwa, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** {{ill|Mikenshō-no-kami|ja|御剣仗神|12=draft}} ** **Omakunari Shrine:** {{ill|Ōwakako-no-mikoto|ja|大若子命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|12=draft}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|12=draft}} 23 (804 CE) ** **Omakunari Shrine:** Traditionally dated to the 21st year of Emperor Yūryaku (477 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|12=draft}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=draft}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=draft}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|12=draft}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=draft}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|12=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|12=draft}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at {{ill|Umenomiya Taisha|ja|梅宮大社|12=draft}} in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=draft}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}}: About 200m south-southeast of {{ill|Yamada Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|12=draft}}<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|12=draft}}. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Omakunari Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kusanagi-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-5 Tokiwa, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** {{ill|Mikenshō-no-kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft}} ** **Omakunari Shrine:** {{ill|Ōwakako-no-mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|qq=draft}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft}} 23 (804 CE) ** **Omakunari Shrine:** Traditionally dated to the 21st year of Emperor Yūryaku (477 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinamisai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at {{ill|Umenomiya Taisha|ja|梅宮大社|qq=draft}} in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft}}: About 200m south-southeast of {{ill|Yamada Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft}}. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at {{ill|Umenomiya Taisha|ja|梅宮大社|qq=draft}} in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft}}: About 200m south-southeast of {{ill|Yamada Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft}}. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Omakunari Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kusanagi-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-5 Tokiwa, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** {{ill|Mikenshō-no-kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft}} ** **Omakunari Shrine:** {{ill|Ōwakako-no-mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|qq=draft}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft}} 23 (804 CE) ** **Omakunari Shrine:** Traditionally dated to the 21st year of Emperor Yūryaku (477 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at {{ill|Umenomiya Taisha|ja|梅宮大社|qq=draft}} in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft}}: About 200m south-southeast of {{ill|Yamada Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft}}<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft}}. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}: About 200m south-southeast of {{ill|Yamada Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Kamiguchi Station|qq=}}<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi Station|qq=}}. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] to [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}・{{ill|Omakunari Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omakunari Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kusanagi-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-5 Tokiwa, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** {{ill|Mikenshō-no-kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikenshō-no-kami|qq=}} ** **Omakunari Shrine:** {{ill|Ōwakako-no-mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako-no-mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku era|qq=}} 23 (804 CE) ** **Omakunari Shrine:** Traditionally dated to the 21st year of Emperor Yūryaku (477 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at {{ill|Umenomiya Taisha|ja|梅宮大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umenomiya Taisha|qq=}} in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}: About 200m south-southeast of {{ill|Yamada Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Kamiguchi Station|qq=}}<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi Station|qq=}}. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}: About 200m south-southeast of {{ill|Yamada Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Kamiguchi Station|qq=}}<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi Station|qq=}}. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}・{{ill|Omakunari Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omakunari Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kusanagi-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-5 Tokiwa, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** {{ill|Mikenshō-no-kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikenshō-no-kami|qq=}} ** **Omakunari Shrine:** {{ill|Ōwakako-no-mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako-no-mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** Before {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku era|qq=}} 23 (804 CE) ** **Omakunari Shrine:** Traditionally dated to the 21st year of Emperor Yūryaku (477 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at {{ill|Umenomiya Taisha|ja|梅宮大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umenomiya Taisha|qq=}} in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}: About 200m south-southeast of {{ill|Yamada Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Kamiguchi Station|qq=}}<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi Station|qq=}}. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.

== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}: About 200m south-southeast of {{ill|Yamada Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Kamiguchi Station|qq=}}<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi Station|qq=}}. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}・{{ill|Omakunari Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omakunari Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kusanagi-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-5 Tokiwa, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** {{ill|Mikenshō-no-kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikenshō-no-kami|qq=}} ** **Omakunari Shrine:** {{ill|Ōwakako-no-mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako-no-mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) ** **Omakunari Shrine:** Traditionally dated to the 21st year of Emperor Yūryaku (477 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.

== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}・{{ill|Omakunari Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omakunari Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kusanagi-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-5 Tokiwa, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** {{ill|Mikenshō-no-kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikenshō-no-kami|qq=}} ** **Omakunari Shrine:** {{ill|Ōwakako-no-mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako-no-mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) ** **Omakunari Shrine:** Traditionally dated to the 21st year of Emperor Yūryaku (477 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 中村精貮『志摩の地名の話』伊勢志摩国立公園協会、昭和26th year 11月3日、167p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.

== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}・{{ill|Omakunari Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omakunari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kusanagi-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-5 Tokiwa, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** {{ill|Mikenshō-no-kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikenshō-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** **Omakunari Shrine:** {{ill|Ōwakako-no-mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako-no-mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) ** **Omakunari Shrine:** Traditionally dated to the 21st year of Emperor Yūryaku (477 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 中村精貮『志摩の地名の話』伊勢志摩国立公園協会、昭和26th year 11月3日、167p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p.
