{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17228323]] Wikidata name: Kusanagi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}・{{ill|Omakunari Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omakunari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kusanagi-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg | location = 1-5 Tokiwa, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495|N|136.696722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** {{ill|Mikenshō-no-kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikenshō-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** **Omakunari Shrine:** {{ill|Ōwakako-no-mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako-no-mikoto|qq=|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111114032}}, {{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) ** **Omakunari Shrine:** Traditionally dated to the 21st year of Emperor Yūryaku (477 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others | location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和44th year 5月20日、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 中村精貮『志摩の地名の話』伊勢志摩国立公園協会、昭和26th year 11月3日、167p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0602.html 草奈伎神社（くさなぎじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0603.html 大間国生神社（おおまくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会

== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 中村精貮『志摩の地名の話』伊勢志摩国立公園協会、昭和26th year 11月3日、167p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17228323]] Wikidata name: Kusanagi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}・{{ill|Omakunari Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omakunari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kusanagi-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg | location = 1-5 Tokiwa, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495|N|136.696722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** {{ill|Mikenshō-no-kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikenshō-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** **Omakunari Shrine:** {{ill|Ōwakako-no-mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako-no-mikoto|qq=|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111114032}}, {{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) ** **Omakunari Shrine:** Traditionally dated to the 21st year of Emperor Yūryaku (477 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others | location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 中村精貮『志摩の地名の話』伊勢志摩国立公園協会、昭和26th year 11月3日、167p. * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}・{{ill|Omakunari Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omakunari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kusanagi-jinja (Geku) 02.jpg | location = 1-5 Tokiwa, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495|N|136.696722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** {{ill|Mikenshō-no-kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikenshō-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** **Omakunari Shrine:** {{ill|Ōwakako-no-mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako-no-mikoto|qq=|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111114032}}, {{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) ** **Omakunari Shrine:** Traditionally dated to the 21st year of Emperor Yūryaku (477 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others | location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

{{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others | location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

{{Coord|34.495|N|136.696722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** {{ill|Mikenshō-no-kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikenshō-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** **Omakunari Shrine:** {{ill|Ōwakako-no-mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako-no-mikoto|qq=|1=Ōwakako no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111114032}}, {{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = ** **Kusanagi Shrine:** Before [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] 23 (804 CE) ** **Omakunari Shrine:** Traditionally dated to the 21st year of Emperor Yūryaku (477 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) on October 19 | Rituals = [[Kinensai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others | location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

{{ill|Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|ja|乙若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuwakako-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others | location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

{{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|qq=|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

{{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}''' (草奈伎神社) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.
Both shrines are regarded as highly prestigious within the Outer Shrine complex. They are part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, the tradition of reconstructing shrine buildings every 20 years, and they host more frequent rituals compared to other auxiliary shrines.<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1969): p. 91.</ref>
== Overview == This section covers common information about {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.
Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

It Ranks first among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 53.</ref>
This article also covers the {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (*Omakunari Jinja*), which shares the same precinct as Kusanagi Shrine.

Located at 1142 Tokiwa 1-chome, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]<ref name="sk39">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213, 299ページ</ref> (registered address: Tokiwa 1-chome, No. 5), the shrines are situated on a plateau known as "Ōmahira"<ref name="gs"/>. Upon entering the shrine grounds through the first torii gate, {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the left (west side) and {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Ōmakunari Shrine|lt_ja=大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} is on the right (east side)<ref name="ic"/>. The shrine grounds cover an area of 1,744 m<sup>2</sup><ref name="sk36">式内社研究会 編（1990）：216, 303ページ</ref>.
No offering box is placed. The shrine is surrounded on three sides by residential areas, with the south side facing a city road in Ise<ref name="sk36"/>.
== Deities == The enshrined deity of Kusunaki Shrine is {{ill|Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja|御剣仗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishirushi-no-Tsurugi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>, considered to be the spirit of the sword used by {{ill|Ōwakako no Mikoto|ja|大若子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwakako no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} who served as priests of the Outer Shrine (Geku)<ref name="gs"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine enshrines the brothers Ōwakako no Mikoto and Otowako no Mikoto<ref name="sk02">式内社研究会 編（1990）：302ページ</ref>. Both deities are also enshrined at [[:en:Umenomiya Taisha|Umenomiya Taisha]] in Kyoto.
== Kusunaki Shrine == The name "Kusunaki" has various historical spellings such as "草奈支" and "草奈岐". Today, it is standardized as "草奈伎" following the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}<ref name="sk3">式内社研究会 編（1990）：213ページ</ref>.
The shrine building is of [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, surrounded by a sacred fence (tamagaki)<ref name="sk7">式内社研究会 編（1990）：217ページ</ref>. It was reconstructed on June 25, 1976<ref name="sk7"/>.
== Ōmakuni Shrine == According to the {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was established in 477 AD<ref name="sk99">式内社研究会 編（1990）：299, 302ページ</ref>. The shrine building is of Shimmei-zukuri style, similar to Kusunaki Shrine<ref name="sk04">式内社研究会 編（1990）：304ページ</ref>. It was rebuilt on May 28, 1976<ref name="sk04"/>.
== History == Both shrines were established before 927 AD, as recorded in the Engishiki. The current location was restored in 1645 through the efforts of priest Watarai Tsunetoki<ref name="sk39"/>.
Ōmakuni Shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Bombing of Ujiyamada|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bombing of Ujiyamada|1=Bombing of Ujiyamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18990749}} in 1945<ref>伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ</ref>.
== Rituals == Kusunaki Shrine conducts all major rituals within the shrine grounds, while Ōmakuni Shrine performs some rituals remotely and others on-site<ref name="sk02"/>.
== Flora == The shrine grounds are home to large trees like zelkova, camphor, and oak, along with smaller shrubs<ref>式内社研究会 編（1990）：216 - 217, 303ページ</ref>.
== Access == * By JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 200m south-southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada Kamiguchi Station]]<ref name="sk39"/>. * By Kintetsu: Approximately 9 minutes on foot from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * By bus: About 2 minutes on foot from "Yamada Kamiguchi" bus stop.

2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社|lt=Kusanagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}・{{ill|Omakunari Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omakunari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrines (*sessha*) | Established = **
